title: Never SALT Again | JotPsych
meta_description: Same-As-Last-Time notes are the most preventable cause of behavioral-health audit recoupments. JotPsych can't write one. By design.
h2: One button. One bad habit. One audit finding.
This is what it costs once they catch it .
The payers themselves have written it down .
Same patient. Same dog. Different note. Every time.
It is not a button. It is the architecture.
The audit letter is not the right time to find out.
h3: OIG flagged cloned notes across patients and across dates. Same clinic. Same physician.
The case where DOJ pointed to the misspelled words.
quotes: "the medical record did not support medical necessity or the level of care billed and there was cloning of documentation for multiple dates of treatment for the same patients and for multiple patients on the same dates of treatment" HHS Office of Inspector General, settlement announcement, April 3, 2019

"Deliberately inaccurate group therapy notes for different patients on different days, often years apart, were in many respects identical, including having the same descriptions of patients' statements in group sessions and even the same misspelled words." U.S. Department of Justice, press release 13-1188
Each provider record should be individualized and unique... Providers are not permitted to clone or copy and paste member or treatment information in provider records.
